title: Aureline typed policy-decision explain packet
description: Boundary schema for the typed explain packet a policy-decision site emits instead of an opaque allow/deny string. Every admin-policy narrowing, workspace-trust restriction, capability-gate denial, entitlement-grace expiry, revocation enforcement, offline degradation, emergency-disable firing, last-known-good fallback, or grace-state transition produces one policy_decision_explain_record so reviewers, support surfaces, and the operator can read a closed decision_class, a closed narrowing_reason, an opaque rule ref, an opaque source ref, an opaque audit event id, the policy epoch, the fallback path the user remains on, the identity-mode and trust-state context the decision ran under, and the local-versus-vendor-console explainability envelope. Raw policy rule bodies, raw OIDC tokens, raw claim payloads, raw tenant directory material, raw bundle bytes, raw signatures, and raw secret material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and reviewable-sentence summaries only. The vocabulary re-exports the identity_mode, deployment_profile_class, workspace_trust_state, redaction_class, and explainability_envelope shapes already frozen in schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json, schemas/identity/entitlement_snapshot.schema.json, and schemas/identity/admin_audit_packet.schema.json. Adding a new decision_class, narrowing_reason, fallback_path_class, provider_chain_source_class, or emergency_disable_source_class value is additive-minor and requires a policy_decision_explain_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by governance_and_policy and security_trust.

policy_decision_explain_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this explain-packet vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in admin exports, support bundles, release evidence, and boundary-manifest rows. Raw rule bodies, raw signatures, raw policy bytes, raw claim payloads, and raw directory attributes never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence safe for banners, admin exports, support packets, and release evidence. Raw policy rule text, raw claim payloads, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

export_safe_label.description: Short, export-safe label for banners and admin consoles. Raw URLs, raw tenant ids, raw directory attribute values, and raw signing material MUST NOT appear.

stable_dot_id.description: Dot-separated stable identifier; used for row ids and decision-site refs.

identity_mode.description: Identity mode re-exported from ADR-0001 and schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json. Every decision records the mode it ran under so later reviewers can reproduce the gate.

deployment_profile_class.description: Deployment-profile class re-exported from the identity family.

workspace_trust_state.description: Workspace trust state re-exported from ADR-0001 and ADR-0018. Policy MAY narrow trusted-workspace behaviour but MUST NOT widen it; the record preserves the state the decision ran under.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from the lifecycle/support/release vocabulary.

policy_epoch_ref.description: Stable epoch identifier of the form policy_epoch.<lane>.<windowed>.<id>, matching schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json. Consumers never concatenate or parse the epoch beyond equality and 'newer than' ordering.

bundle_version_ref.description: Bundle version identifier of the form <bundle_name>@<semver>, matching schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json.

decision_class.description: Closed decision-class vocabulary. admin_policy_narrowing_refused_as_widening names the case where a bundle attempted to widen a non-widenable surface and the client refused; emergency_disable_fired names a kill-switch firing that ratchets for the current session; provider_chain_fell_back_to_embedded_default names a resolution that bottomed out on the compiled default because every higher layer was unavailable.

decision_outcome_class.description: Closed outcome vocabulary. The record separates decision_class (what kind of decision this was) from decision_outcome_class (what the operator experiences). allowed_with_note is admissible only when the decision_class is entitlement_grant, entitlement_grace_active, or feature_flag_resolution_narrowed with a reviewable_sentence note.

narrowing_reason.description: Closed narrowing-reason vocabulary. The set re-exports vocabulary already frozen in schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json and schemas/security/*.schema.json so policy-decision sites and admin-audit sites speak the same reason language.

fallback_path_class.description: Closed fallback-path vocabulary. repair_hook_visible_no_silent_fallback is the value required when the decision fails closed and must surface a visible repair affordance; none_surface_unavailable_with_reason is admissible only when the record also carries a reviewable unavailable_reason and an opaque audit_event_id.

provider_chain_source_class.description: Closed provider-chain source vocabulary. Mirrors artifacts/governance/feature_flag_provider_chain.yaml so a decision record names the resolving layer directly.

local_vs_vendor_console_explainability_c.description: Closed local-versus-vendor-console vocabulary, re-exported from schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json. locally_explainable_from_bundle is the default and the value required for every enterprise narrowing claim.

locally_inspectable_fields.description: Fields a local inspector, CLI, support-export surface, or admin desktop can render from this record without calling the vendor console.

vendor_console_only_fields.description: Fields only the vendor console can render at full detail. MUST be an empty array when explainability_class is locally_explainable_from_bundle.

acknowledged_gap_waiver_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/governance/exception_packet rows acknowledging an explainability gap. MUST be non-empty when explainability_class is vendor_console_only_acknowledged_gap.

site_id.description: Stable site identifier, e.g. site.settings.experiment_rollout_layer or site.capability_gate.save_path.

site_kind.description: Kind of site that produced this record. The site_kind MUST be consistent with the decision_class; e.g. emergency_disable_handler pairs with decision_class emergency_disable_fired.

surface_label.description: Short, export-safe label for the surface the decision rendered into (banner, admin export, support packet, CLI stdout, etc.).

rule_id.description: Opaque, stable id for the rule or policy clause that produced the decision. MUST NOT contain raw rule body text or raw directory attribute values.

rule_family.description: Closed rule-family vocabulary. rule_family lets reviewers locate the originating contract (admin policy, capability lifecycle, entitlement plan, feature-flag rollout, kill switch, signature validator, offline-grace handler, provider-chain resolver).

rule_note.description: Short, reviewable summary of the rule, safe for banners and admin exports. Raw rule body text MUST NOT appear.

source_kind.description: Closed source-kind vocabulary. The source_kind names which upstream record or invocation materialised the decision.

source_id.description: Opaque id into the named source. For policy_bundle_record and entitlement_snapshot_record this is the record's opaque id; for feature_flag_provider_chain_profile this is the profile_id.

bundle_version_ref.description: Present only when source_kind is policy_bundle_record.

reason_class.description: Closed unavailable-reason vocabulary, mirroring artifacts/governance/feature_flag_provider_chain.yaml#unavailable_reason_classes and extending with identity-mode / trust-state incompatibility.

record_id.description: Stable, opaque record id. Record ids stay stable across redaction-class changes and surface projections.

emergency_disable_source.description: Present only when decision_class is emergency_disable_fired. Names the kill-switch source that fired.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch the decision resolved under. When the decision did not consult a policy bundle (embedded-default resolution, local CLI invocation, CI workflow input), the epoch still records the last known epoch so reviewers can reproduce the gate.

unavailable_reason.description: Present only when fallback_path is none_surface_unavailable_with_reason or decision_outcome is denied with no recovery path.

audit_event_id.description: Opaque id into the admin_audit_packet_record family so every decision can be joined to the audit trail. Raw audit bodies never appear here; consumers dereference opaquely.

summary_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence summarising the decision for banners, admin exports, and support packets. Raw policy rule bodies, raw claim payloads, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

allOf.description: Emergency-disable firings MUST name the kill-switch source and the ratchet-session fallback.
When fallback_path is none_surface_unavailable_with_reason, the record MUST carry an unavailable_reason.
vendor_console_only_acknowledged_gap requires at least one waiver ref.

policy_decision_explain_record.description: One typed explain record per policy-decision site invocation. Records are append-only; a later decision at the same site mints a new record id and a new timestamp rather than overwriting the previous one.
